seo.title: Context Window Management for AI Agents in Semantic Kernel Agents
seo.description: How to detect and fix context window management for ai agents in Semantic Kernel agents. Framework-specific detection, fixes, and Vex automation.

content.intro: In Semantic Kernel, Context Window Management issues for AI Agents arise when plugin chaining, planners, and memory systems exceed token limits during execution, leading to truncated prompts and degraded performance. Agents fail silently as the Kernel's function invocation pipeline doesn't natively track cumulative context size across chained operations. This manifests across Python and TypeScript implementations through cascading errors in multi-step reasoning and hallucinated memory recalls.

howItManifests.symptom: Subsequent plugins receive truncated context from previous function results
howItManifests.impact: Agent produces incoherent responses after 3-4 plugin invocations

howItManifests.symptom: Handlebars/SequentialPlanner selects too many functions exceeding window
howItManifests.impact: Runtime token limit errors during plan execution

howItManifests.symptom: VolatileMemoryStore + embedding recall pulls excessive chat history
howItManifests.impact: Recent interactions overshadowed by irrelevant historical context

howItManifests.symptom: KernelFunctionContext accumulates state across unrelated plugin calls
howItManifests.impact: Agents mix data from different user sessions/conversations

detection.name: Token Overflow Monitor
detection.description: Intercepts KernelFunction invocation to track cumulative context tokens before LLM calls
detection.code:
from semantic_kernel import Kernel
from semantic_kernel.contents import ChatHistory

def token_overflow_filter(function_result: str, context: Kernel):
    chat_history = context['chat_history']
    total_tokens = sum(len(msg.content) for msg in chat_history) // 4  # rough token estimate
    if total_tokens > 12000:
        raise ValueError(f"Context overflow: {total_tokens} tokens")
    return function_result

kernel = Kernel()
kernel.filters.add('post_function', token_overflow_filter)

detection.name: Plan Length Validator
detection.description: Validates SequentialPlanner output against token budget before execution
detection.code:
from semantic_kernel.planning import SequentialPlanner
from semantic_kernel.contents import ChatHistory

def validate_plan(plan_steps, kernel):
    estimated_tokens = 0
    for step in plan_steps:
        estimated_tokens += len(str(step)) // 4
    if estimated_tokens > 11000:
        raise ValueError(f"Plan exceeds token limit: {estimated_tokens}")
    return plan_steps

planner = SequentialPlanner(kernel)
plan = planner.create_plan(goal)
validate_plan(plan._steps, kernel)

detection.name: Context Size Logger
detection.description: Logs context window usage patterns across agent sessions
detection.code:
import logging
from semantic_kernel import Kernel

class ContextLogger:
    def __init__(self):
        self.session_context = []
    
    def log_context(self, context: Kernel):
        ctx_size = len(str(context))
        self.session_context.append(ctx_size)
        logging.info(f"Context size: {ctx_size}, Session avg: {sum(self.session_context)/len(self.session_context):.0f}")
        return context

logger = ContextLogger()
kernel.add_filter('pre_function', logger.log_context)

fixes.name: Dynamic Context Truncation
fixes.description: Automatically prunes chat history to fit token window using embedding similarity
fixes.code:
from semantic_kernel.memory import SemanticTextMemory
from semantic_kernel.contents import ChatHistory

def truncate_context(chat_history: ChatHistory, max_tokens: int = 10000):
    if sum(len(msg.content) for msg in chat_history) // 4 < max_tokens:
        return chat_history
    
    # Keep last N messages + most relevant by recency
    recent = chat_history[-5:]
    memory = kernel.memory
    relevant = memory.search('collection', query, limit=3, min_relevance_score=0.7)
    
    truncated = ChatHistory()
    truncated.add_system_message(chat_history[0].content)  # Keep system prompt
    truncated.add_user_messages(recent + relevant)
    return truncated

# Usage
context['chat_history'] = truncate_context(context['chat_history'])

fixes.name: Token-Aware Planner
fixes.description: Custom planner that estimates token cost before generating execution plans
fixes.code:
from semantic_kernel.planning import Planner
from semantic_kernel.functions import KernelFunction

class TokenAwarePlanner(SequentialPlanner):
    def create_plan(self, goal: str):
        functions = self._kernel.functions.list_functions()
        # Estimate token cost
        viable_functions = [f for f in functions 
                          if self.estimate_tokens(f) < 2000]
        
        plan = super().create_plan(goal)
        if self.plan_token_cost(plan) > 11000:
            plan._steps = plan._steps[:3]  # Truncate expensive plan
        return plan
    
    def estimate_tokens(self, func: KernelFunction) -> int:
        return len(func.describe()) // 4 + 500  # function desc + output

planner = TokenAwarePlanner(kernel)

fixes.name: Session Context Partitioning
fixes.description: Partitions long conversations into token-bounded sessions

vexAutomation.heading: Automating Context Window Management for AI Agents Detection with Vex
vexAutomation.description:
Vex integrates as a Semantic Kernel filter that automatically monitors context window usage across all function invocations, planner executions, and memory recalls. It establishes token budgets per session and flags violations in real-time, providing detailed traces of which plugins or planning steps caused overflow. 

The Vex SDK (`pip install semantic-kernel vex-sdk`) adds zero-config monitoring that captures cascading failures from plugin chaining and invalid plans. Vex's ML-powered analysis correlates context growth patterns with specific failure modes, automatically suggesting optimal truncation points and plan revisions. Remediation runs generate fixed execution traces with 95%+ success rates on context-bound failures.

Vex dashboards provide per-agent token utilization heatmaps and auto-generates context management policies tuned to your specific plugin ecosystem.
vexAutomation.code:
from semantic_kernel import Kernel
import vex

# Install: pip install semantic-kernel vex-sdk
kernel = Kernel()

# Vex auto-integration as Kernel filter
@vex.monitor_kernel(kernel)
def agent_pipeline(goal: str):
    planner = SequentialPlanner(kernel)
    plan = planner.create_plan(goal)
    result = plan.invoke_async(kernel)
    return result.result

# Vex auto-detects context overflow, invalid plans, memory hallucination
# Provides auto-remediation with truncation + replanning

# View issues: vex dashboard
# Auto-fix deployment: vex apply-remediations
